 {"id":"gt-4u5z.1","title":"Update rig init to create refinery as worktree","description":"Modify rig initialization to create refinery as a git worktree instead of a separate clone.

## Changes
- internal/rig/init.go or similar - change refinery setup
- Create worktree at <rig>/refinery/ with branch 'refinery'
- Remove clone logic for refinery
- Update any path assumptions

## Considerations
- Refinery worktree should track main (or its own branch?)
- May need to handle existing rigs (migration path)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:25.927013-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T20:31:07.100875-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T20:31:07.100875-08:00","close_reason":"Rig init now creates refinery as worktree of mayor clone","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-4u5z.1","depends_on_id":"gt-4u5z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:25.92745-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-4u5z.2","title":"Remove origin push from polecat workflow","description":"Polecats should no longer push their branches to origin.

## Changes
- internal/cmd/done.go - remove 'git push origin HEAD'
- internal/cmd/mq_submit.go - remove branch push
- Molecule templates - remove push instructions
- Any hooks that push polecat branches

## Verification
- Polecat completes work without pushing
- Branch stays local
- Refinery can still see the branch (via shared .git)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:27.079456-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T20:33:43.173059-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T20:33:43.173059-08:00","close_reason":"Removed origin push from done.go, mq_submit.go, spawn.go, and molecule templates","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-4u5z.2","depends_on_id":"gt-4u5z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:27.081989-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-4u5z.2","depends_on_id":"gt-4u5z.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:43.547484-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"gt-4u5z.3","title":"Update refinery to read local branches","description":"Refinery manager needs to access polecat branches locally instead of fetching from origin.

## Changes
- internal/refinery/manager.go - remove origin fetch for MR branches
- internal/refinery/engineer.go - local branch access
- Remove DeleteRemoteBranch calls (branches are local now)
- Update merge workflow to work with local refs

## Key insight
All worktrees share the same .git - refinery can see polecat/nux branch directly.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:28.500632-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T20:34:33.879575-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T20:34:33.879575-08:00","close_reason":"Refinery now deletes local branches instead of remote branches","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-4u5z.3","depends_on_id":"gt-4u5z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:28.502671-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-4u5z.3","depends_on_id":"gt-4u5z.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:43.633822-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"gt-4u5z.4","title":"Convert MR queue to wisp storage","description":"MR queue should use wisp storage (.beads-wisp/) instead of durable beads.

## Design (Simplified)

With refinery as worktree, polecats and refinery share the same machine. No cross-machine MR coordination needed. Wisps are the natural fit.

### Storage
- Location: `.beads-wisp/mq/<mr-id>.json`
- Format: Same MRFields structure (branch, target, source_issue, worker, rig)
- Lifecycle: Create on submit, delete after merge

### Code Changes

1. **MR Creation** (done.go, mq_submit.go)
   - Write to `.beads-wisp/mq/` instead of `bd create --type=merge-request`
   - Generate ID: `mr-<timestamp>-<random>` or similar

2. **Refinery Query** (refinery/manager.go, engineer.go)
   - Read from `.beads-wisp/mq/` directory
   - List files, parse JSON, process in order

3. **Cleanup**
   - Delete MR file after successful merge
   - On failure, leave file (refinery retries)

### What Stays in Beads
- Source issues (the work being merged) - still tracked in beads
- Merge commits reference source issue ID
- `bd close <issue>` still happens on merge

### Benefits
- No sync overhead for transient MR state
- Simpler model (file per MR, delete when done)
- Consistent with wisp philosophy (ephemeral operational state)

### Not Needed
- Digest squashing (MRs are deleted, not accumulated)
- Cross-machine visibility (single-machine architecture)","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:29.716788-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T21:18:03.969038-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-4u5z.4","depends_on_id":"gt-4u5z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:29.717201-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-4u5z.4","depends_on_id":"gt-4u5z.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:43.719801-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

- Refinery is worktree, not clone
- Polecat branches stay local
- Only main pushed to origin
- MRs are wisps (ephemeral)","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:31.192025-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:31.192025-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-4u5z.5","depends_on_id":"gt-4u5z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:31.194085-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-4u5z.5","depends_on_id":"gt-4u5z.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:43.819111-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-4u5z.5","depends_on_id":"gt-4u5z.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:43.907113-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-4u5z.5","depends_on_id":"gt-4u5z.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:43.99734-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"gt-4u5z.4","title":"Convert MR queue to wisp storage","description":"MR queue should use wisp storage (.beads-wisp/) instead of durable beads.

## Design (Simplified)

With refinery as worktree, polecats and refinery share the same machine. No cross-machine MR coordination needed. Wisps are the natural fit.

### Storage
- Location: `.beads-wisp/mq/<mr-id>.json`
- Format: Same MRFields structure (branch, target, source_issue, worker, rig)
- Lifecycle: Create on submit, delete after merge

### Code Changes

1. **MR Creation** (done.go, mq_submit.go)
   - Write to `.beads-wisp/mq/` instead of `bd create --type=merge-request`
   - Generate ID: `mr-<timestamp>-<random>` or similar

2. **Refinery Query** (refinery/manager.go, engineer.go)
   - Read from `.beads-wisp/mq/` directory
   - List files, parse JSON, process in order

3. **Cleanup**
   - Delete MR file after successful merge
   - On failure, leave file (refinery retries)

### What Stays in Beads
- Source issues (the work being merged) - still tracked in beads
- Merge commits reference source issue ID
- `bd close <issue>` still happens on merge

### Benefits
- No sync overhead for transient MR state
- Simpler model (file per MR, delete when done)
- Consistent with wisp philosophy (ephemeral operational state)

### Not Needed
- Digest squashing (MRs are deleted, not accumulated)
- Cross-machine visibility (single-machine architecture)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T20:25:29.716788-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T21:22:32.180049-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T21:22:32.180049-08:00","close_reason":"MR queue now uses wisp storage (.beads-wisp/mq/).  {"id":"gt-gby","title":"gt handoff: Unified agent lifecycle command","description":"## Summary

Unified `gt handoff` command for ALL agent types to request lifecycle actions.

## Usage

gt handoff              # Context-aware default
gt handoff --shutdown   # Terminate, cleanup, don't restart
gt handoff --cycle      # Restart with handoff mail
gt handoff --restart    # Fresh restart, no handoff

## Context-Aware Defaults

| Agent Type | Default | Reason |
|------------|---------|--------|
| Polecat | --shutdown | Ephemeral, work is done |
| Witness | --cycle | Long-running, context full |
| Refinery | --cycle | Long-running, context full |
| Mayor | --cycle | Long-running, context full |
| Crew | (sends mail only) | Human-managed |

## What gt handoff Does

1. **Verify safe to stop**
   - Git state clean (no uncommitted changes)
   - Work handed off (PR exists for polecats)

2. **Send handoff mail to self** (for cycle/restart)
   - Captures current state
   - New session will read this

3. **Send lifecycle request to manager**
   - Polecats/Refinery → Witness
   - Witness/Mayor → Daemon
   - Format: mail to <manager> with action type

4. **Set state: requesting_<action>**
   - Lifecycle manager checks this before acting

5. **Wait for termination**
   - Don't self-exit - let manager kill session
   - Ensures clean handoff

## Lifecycle Request Flow

Agent                           Lifecycle Manager
  |                                    |
  | 1. gt handoff --cycle              |
  |    a. Verify git clean             |
  |    b. Send handoff mail to self    |
  |    c. Set requesting_cycle=true    |
  |    d. Send lifecycle request       |
  |------------------------------------→|
  |                                    |
  |                   2. Receive request
  |                   3. Verify state  |
  |                   4. Kill session  |
  |                   5. Start new     |
  |                      (for cycle)   |
  |                                    |
  |         New session reads handoff  |
  |         Resumes work               |

## Who Manages Whom

| Agent | Sends lifecycle request to |
|-------|---------------------------|
| Polecat | <rig>/witness |
| Refinery | <rig>/witness |
| Witness | daemon/ |
| Mayor | daemon/ |

## Implementation

1. Detect current role (polecat, witness, refinery, mayor, crew)
2. Apply context-aware default if no flag specified
3. Run pre-flight checks (git clean, work handed off)
4. Send handoff mail to self (if cycling)
5. Send lifecycle request to appropriate manager
6. Set requesting_<action> in state.json
7. Wait (manager will kill us)

## For Polecats (--shutdown)

Additional cleanup after kill:
- Witness removes worktree
- Witness deletes polecat branch
- Polecat ceases to exist

## Related Issues

- gt-99m: Daemon (handles Mayor/Witness lifecycle)
- gt-7ik: Ephemeral polecats (polecat cleanup)
- gt-eu9: Witness session cycling","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-18T11:39:40.806863-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-18T18:18:22.35369-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-gby","depends_on_id":"gt-7ik","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T11:39:46.423945-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-gby","depends_on_id":"gt-eu9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T11:39:46.547204-08:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"gt-gby","depends_on_id":"gt-99m","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-18T11:50:24.142182-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"gt-gl2","title":"Clarify Mayor vs Witness cleanup responsibilities","description":"Document the cleanup authority model: Witness owns ALL per-worker cleanup, Mayor never involved.

## The Rule

**Witness handles ALL per-worker cleanup. Mayor is never involved.**

## Why This Matters

1. Separation of concerns: Mayor strategic, Witness operational
2. Reduced coordination overhead: No back-and-forth for routine cleanup
3. Faster shutdown: Witness kills workers immediately upon verification
4. Cleaner escalation: Mayor only hears about problems

## What Witness Handles

- Verifying worker git state before kill
- Nudging workers to fix dirty state
- Killing worker sessions
- Updating worker state (sleep/wake)
- Logging verification results

## What Mayor Handles

- Receiving swarm complete notifications
- Deciding whether to start new swarms
- Handling escalations (stuck workers after 3 retries)
- Cross-rig coordination

## Escalation Path

Worker stuck -> Witness nudges (up to 3x) -> Witness escalates to Mayor -> Mayor decides: force kill, reassign, or human

## Anti-Patterns

DO NOT: Mayor asks Witness if worker X is clean
DO: Witness reports swarm complete, all workers verified

DO NOT: Mayor kills worker sessions directly
DO: Mayor tells Witness to abort swarm, Witness handles cleanup

DO NOT: Workers report done to Mayor
DO: Workers report to Witness, Witness aggregates and reports up","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:48:56.678724-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-15T20:48:12.068964-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-gl2","depends_on_id":"gt-82y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-15T19:49:05.929877-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
+{"id":"gt-gl6s","title":"gt spawn: polecats report success but no work actually happens","description":"## Problem

gt spawn appears to succeed (creates worktree, sends mail notifications, reports 'Session started') but:
1. No actual Claude session runs
2. Polecats send POLECAT_DONE messages without doing work
3. No code is committed or pushed
4. MR issues are created but branches don't exist

## Observed Behavior

Spawned 12 polecats with gt spawn beads/<name> --create --issue <id>
- All reported 'Session started'
- All sent POLECAT_STARTED notifications
- All sent POLECAT_DONE notifications
- But: no worktrees remained, no branches pushed, no code written

## Expected Behavior

Polecats should actually run Claude Code and complete assigned work before sending POLECAT_DONE.

## Impact

Silent failure causes false completion signals, wasted witness time processing fake completions, and stale MR/assignee pollution in beads.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-23T21:22:45.498303-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T21:22:45.498303-08:00"}

 {"id":"gt-gswn","title":"Integration test: agent waits for CI via gate","description":"End-to-end test of the gate workflow.

## Test Scenario
1. Agent creates gate: bd gate create --await gh:run:123 --timeout 5m --notify beads/dave
2. Agent writes handoff and exits
3. Deacon patrol checks gate condition
4. (Mock) GitHub run completes
5. Deacon notifies waiter and closes gate
6. New agent session reads mail and resumes

## Test Requirements
- Mock GitHub API responses
- Test timeout path
- Test multiple waiters
- Verify mail notifications sent

## Moved from beads
Originally bd-rl5t. Tests Deacon patrol which is in gastown.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-23T12:23:56.582834-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T12:23:56.582834-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"gt-gswn","depends_on_id":"gt-dh65","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-23T12:24:01.787444-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
